title: clematis bojeri var. macrophylla
intro: Clematis bojeri var. macrophylla, also known as the giant clematis, is a species of flowering plant in the clematis genus. It is a perennial climber native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
